test_cases.description: Valid API response with users; CSV is generated and saved
test_cases.name: successful_fetch_and_save_csv

test_setup.given: API returns an array of valid user objects including location 'Boone'
test_setup.then: CSV file is generated with all fields, filtered for location='Boone', and saved to data/users.csv

test_cases.description: API returns valid users but none with location 'Boone'; CSV file should not be created
test_cases.name: successful_fetch_no_boone_users

test_setup.given: API returns an array of users but none contain location 'Boone'
test_setup.then: No CSV file is created and system signals no matching data

test_cases.description: API returns an empty array; no CSV should be created
test_cases.name: response_empty_array

test_setup.given: API responds with HTTP 200 but body is an empty array
test_setup.then: System throws an error and does not create or modify the CSV file

test_cases.description: API returns 404 Not Found
test_cases.name: api_failure_404

test_setup.then: Function throws an error and no CSV file is created

test_cases.description: Server error during API call
test_cases.name: api_failure_500

test_setup.then: Function throws an error and no CSV file is written

test_cases.description: API returns non-parseable JSON body
test_cases.name: malformed_json_response

test_setup.given: fetch returns OK status but JSON parsing fails
test_setup.then: Error is thrown and no CSV is created

test_cases.description: User objects missing some fields; CSV should include headers for all fields and blank values where missing
test_cases.name: partial_user_objects_missing_fields

test_setup.given: Users contain missing optional fields but location='Boone'
test_setup.then: CSV file is created with blanks for missing values, preserving field names exactly

test_cases.description: API returns exactly one user object; CSV should contain one data row
test_cases.name: single_user_record

test_setup.given: API provides one user with location='Boone'
test_setup.then: CSV file is created with a single data row

test_cases.description: Data directory does not exist; system must create it
test_cases.name: directory_creation_required

test_setup.then: Directory is created and CSV file is successfully written
test_setup.when: main() is called with valid API response including Boone

test_cases.description: Filesystem prevents directory creation
test_cases.name: directory_creation_failure

test_setup.given: data/ directory cannot be created due to permission issues
test_setup.then: System throws an error and no CSV is written

test_cases.description: Write operation to file system fails
test_cases.name: csv_write_failure

test_setup.given: File write operation fails due to lack of write permission
test_setup.then: System throws an error and no CSV file is created
test_setup.when: main() is called with valid API data

test_cases.description: API returns a large array of users; system must handle iteration and CSV creation correctly
test_cases.name: large_user_dataset

test_setup.given: API returns a dataset of thousands of users including Boone
test_setup.then: System successfully generates the large CSV without truncation or failure

test_cases.description: User fields contain commas, newlines, or quotes requiring CSV escaping
test_cases.name: special_characters_in_user_fields

test_setup.given: API response includes Boone user records with special characters
test_setup.then: System escapes fields properly and CSV remains valid

test_cases.description: API returns extra fields not previously known; system should include them as columns
test_cases.name: unexpected_additional_fields_in_response

test_setup.given: API returns user objects with new fields including Boone
test_setup.then: System includes all fields and preserves field names exactly

test_cases.description: Fetch throws a network-level exception; system must error and avoid writing CSV
test_cases.name: network_timeout_or_fetch_exception

test_setup.given: The fetch call throws due to network timeout or connectivity issue
test_setup.then: System throws an error and does not create or modify any CSV file

test_cases.description: Existing CSV file must not be modified when API request or processing fails
test_cases.name: existing_csv_not_modified_on_failure

test_setup.given: An existing data/users.csv file is present and API returns an error
test_setup.then: The existing CSV file remains unchanged

test_cases.description: Location field exists but is not a string or does not match expected structure
test_cases.name: invalid_location_field_structure

test_setup.then: System identifies no matching Boone users and does not create a CSV

test_cases.description: Users array contains mixed valid and invalid user objects; only valid Boone users are processed
test_cases.name: mixed_valid_and_invalid_user_objects

test_setup.given: API returns some valid user objects and some malformed ones, with at least one valid Boone user
test_setup.then: System filters, skips malformed entries during CSV conversion, and writes CSV with only valid Boone users

test_cases.description: Users array contains non-object entries; system must safely ignore them

test_setup.given: API returns array items that are not objects (e.g., null, numbers, strings)
test_setup.then: System ignores invalid entries; if Boone users exist among valid ones, CSV is still generated

test_cases.description: CSV header ordering must match API field ordering as received

test_setup.given: API returns users with fields in a certain order and includes Boone users
test_setup.then: CSV header row preserves the exact field order from API

test_cases.description: Field values include leading/trailing spaces; CSV must preserve values without trimming

test_setup.given: API returns Boone user fields containing leading or trailing spaces

test_cases.description: Verifies that the function correctly converts a valid CSV string with a header and multiple rows into JSON objects.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_happy_path_multiple_rows

test_setup.given: A CSV string with header 'id,name,email' and two valid data rows.
test_setup.then: An array of two JSON objects is returned with keys mapped to header names.
test_setup.when: csvToJson is called with the CSV string.

test_cases.description: Ensures the function correctly parses a CSV with a header and a single data row.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_single_row

test_setup.given: A CSV string with one header line and one data row.
test_setup.then: Returns an array containing a single mapped JSON object.

test_cases.description: Function should return an empty array when the CSV input is an empty string.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_empty_string

test_setup.then: Returns an empty array.

test_cases.description: Function should return an empty array when only header exists but no data rows.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_only_header_no_rows

test_setup.given: A CSV string containing only one header line.
test_setup.then: Returns an empty array because there are less than 2 non-empty lines.

test_cases.description: Ensures trailing empty lines are ignored.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_header_with_trailing_newlines

test_setup.given: A CSV string with one header and multiple blank lines following.
test_setup.then: Returns an empty array because only one valid line exists.

test_cases.description: Ensures whitespace-only lines are treated as empty and ignored.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_whitespace_lines

test_setup.given: A CSV containing header, whitespace-only lines, and one valid row.
test_setup.then: Returns one JSON object mapped correctly to the header.

test_cases.description: If a row has more values than headers, extra values should be ignored based on mapping logic.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_misaligned_columns_extra_values

test_setup.then: Only 'id' and 'name' are mapped and returned; 'Extra' is ignored.

test_cases.description: If a row has fewer values than headers, missing keys should be assigned empty strings or undefined behavior based on mapping logic.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_misaligned_columns_missing_values

test_setup.then: Returned object includes id='1', name='John', and email set to an empty string.

test_cases.description: Ensures special characters in CSV fields (commas inside quotes) are properly parsed.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_special_characters_in_values

test_setup.then: Value for name is 'John, Doe'.

test_cases.description: Ensures Unicode characters are parsed correctly.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_handles_unicode

test_setup.given: CSV containing Unicode characters like emojis or accented letters.
test_setup.then: Returned JSON correctly preserves all characters.

test_cases.description: Ensures headers with leading/trailing spaces are treated as literal header keys.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_headers_with_spaces

test_setup.given: Header ' id , name ' with spaced values.
test_setup.then: Returned JSON uses exact spaced header keys.

test_cases.description: Ensures value trimming does not occur unless implemented.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_rows_with_spaces

test_setup.then: Spaces are preserved in returned JSON.

test_cases.description: Handles a CSV with no commas; each row becomes a single-column value.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_invalid_csv_no_commas

test_setup.then: Returns JSON array with one object { id: '123' }.

test_cases.description: Ensures a CSV consisting entirely of empty or whitespace lines returns an empty array.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_all_empty_lines

test_setup.then: Returns an empty array.

test_cases.description: Verifies performance and correctness with a large CSV dataset.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_handles_large_csv

test_setup.given: A CSV with thousands of rows.
test_setup.then: Returns JSON array with exactly the same number of objects as rows.

test_cases.description: Verifies behavior when duplicate headers exist.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_duplicate_headers

test_setup.then: Later duplicate keys overwrite earlier ones in returned JSON.

test_cases.description: Ensures behavior when header exists but is an empty string.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_header_empty_string

test_setup.then: Returns array with one object containing a single key '' mapped to 'test'.

test_cases.description: Ensures rows that contain empty comma-separated values map to empty strings.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_row_empty_values

test_setup.then: Object returned is { a:'', b:'', c:'' }.

test_cases.description: Handles quoted values containing newline characters.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_quoted_multiline_value

test_setup.then: JSON preserves embedded newline inside value.

test_cases.description: Ensures CSV using Windows CRLF line endings is parsed correctly.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_handles_crlf_newlines

test_setup.given: A CSV where lines end with \r\n.
test_setup.then: Lines are split correctly and mapped into JSON objects.

test_cases.description: Ensures UTF–8 BOM-prefixed CSV headers are handled.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_bom_header_support

test_setup.given: A CSV whose header begins with a UTF–8 BOM character.
test_setup.then: The BOM is treated as part of the header key and mapped literally.

test_cases.description: Checks behavior when a data row contains unmatched quotes.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_unbalanced_quotes

test_setup.given: A CSV row such as '1,"John Doe'.
test_setup.then: Row is parsed literally based on split logic and returned without special quote handling.

test_cases.description: Ensures function handles non-string input safely.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_non_string_input

test_setup.given: Input is not a string (e.g., null or number).
test_setup.then: Function returns an empty array based on failure to split into valid lines.

test_cases.description: Ensures the output JSON array preserves the row order from the CSV.
test_cases.name: csv_to_json_preserves_row_order

test_setup.given: CSV with header 'id' and rows '1' then '2'.
test_setup.then: Returned array is [{id:'1'}, {id:'2'}] in the same order.

test_cases.description: Verify that the endpoint returns parsed JSON when users.csv exists and contains valid data.
test_cases.name: get_users_happy_path_csv_exists

test_setup.given: A valid users.csv file exists with properly formatted CSV content.
test_setup.then: The service reads the file, converts it to JSON, and returns HTTP 200 with the JSON array.

test_cases.description: Verify that the endpoint returns 404 when the users.csv file does not exist.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_missing

test_setup.given: No users.csv file exists in the storage path.
test_setup.then: The service returns HTTP 404 with an error JSON payload.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when users.csv exists but contains only headers and no data rows.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_empty_file

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but contains only column headers.
test_setup.then: The system returns an empty JSON array.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when users.csv exists but contains corrupted or unparsable data.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_corrupted_content

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but contains malformed CSV content that csvToJson cannot parse.
test_setup.then: The system returns a 500 internal error indicating parsing failure.

test_cases.description: Verify that the endpoint can handle a very large CSV file and return large JSON arrays.
test_cases.name: get_users_large_csv_boundary_test

test_setup.given: users.csv exists with thousands of rows.
test_setup.then: The system processes and returns the large JSON response without errors.

test_cases.description: Simulate slow I/O response but valid CSV. Ensure timeout or successful response.
test_cases.name: get_users_slow_file_read

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but disk access is slow.
test_setup.then: The system eventually returns valid JSON without timeout.

test_cases.description: Ensure CSV parser handles whitespace, extra newlines, and returns clean JSON.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_with_extra_whitespace

test_setup.given: CSV contains trailing spaces and blank lines.
test_setup.then: CSV is parsed cleanly and JSON output is correct.

test_cases.description: Ensure system does not process files other than users.csv.
test_cases.name: get_users_non_csv_file_present

test_setup.given: Only other file types exist (e.g., users.txt) but not users.csv.
test_setup.then: System returns 404 file not found.

test_cases.description: Validate that saved CSV (created earlier by other process) respects business rule: only users from Boone are included.
test_cases.name: get_users_filtered_data_context_check

test_setup.given: CSV was generated earlier by filtering API users with location Boone.
test_setup.then: Returned content only includes Boone users.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when users.csv exists but the system cannot read it due to permission issues.
test_cases.name: get_users_file_permission_error

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but file permissions prevent the service from reading it.
test_setup.then: System returns HTTP 500 with an appropriate error message.

test_cases.description: Verify that extra columns in the CSV do not break parsing and are included in JSON output.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_with_extra_unexpected_columns

test_setup.given: users.csv contains all expected columns plus additional ones.
test_setup.then: System returns JSON including all additional CSV columns.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when users.csv exists but headers are missing or incomplete.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_missing_headers

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but does not contain valid header row.
test_setup.then: System returns HTTP 500 due to invalid CSV structure.

test_cases.description: Verify system response when an unsupported HTTP method is used.
test_cases.name: get_users_invalid_http_method

test_setup.given: Client attempts to POST to GET-only endpoint.
test_setup.then: System returns HTTP 405.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when CSV contains rows with missing values.
test_cases.name: get_users_csv_with_missing_values

test_setup.given: users.csv contains some fields left empty.
test_setup.then: System parses missing values as null.
